meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Use this SaaS starter kit guide to ship your MVP faster with Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for beginner founders.
content.answer_block: The root cause of project abandonment is 'feature creep' and technical friction. A SaaS starter kit eliminates setup overhead by providing pre-built auth, payments, and database schemas. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you transition from 'thinking about building' to shipping a production-ready MVP in hours.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned side projects is paved with good intentions and broken environment configurations. You start with a vision of a million-dollar SaaS, only to get buried under the manual labor of setting up Stripe webhooks, database migrations, and authentication flows. The spark dies, and your code sits idle in a private repository, a monument to what could have been.

But the game has changed. By utilizing a high-velocity SaaS starter kit, you bypass the 'setup tax' that kills most projects. We are moving away from manual boilerplate and into an era of AI-assisted assembly, where you act as the architect and your tools handle the heavy lifting. It is time to stop tweaking your config files and start securing your first paying customer.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing code, define the specific problem your SaaS solves. This prevents scope creep by narrowing your focus to a single core feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this business idea: [Insert Idea]. Generate a lean database schema and a core feature list that allows for a 48-hour MVP launch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md outlining the MVP scope and core user flow.'

steps.title: Initialize SaaS Starter Boilerplate
steps.description: Use a production-grade starter kit to gain immediate access to Auth, Payments, and Database ORM.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a Next.js + Supabase + Stripe starter boilerplate. Include user roles and subscription management logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the starter kit and set up the development environment dependencies.'

steps.title: Implement Core Business Logic
steps.description: Focus exclusively on the unique value your product provides, using the starter kit to handle the plumbing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft the backend logic for [Core Feature]. Ensure it integrates with the existing Supabase auth and Stripe subscription check.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core business logic module and associated database migrations.'

steps.title: Build the User Interface
steps.description: Utilize existing component libraries within your starter kit to build high-converting landing pages.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-converting landing page component using Tailwind CSS that highlights the primary pain point and our solution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the landing page components and integrate them into the app router.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push to production immediately. Validation only happens when real users interact with your interface.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment checklist for Vercel, including environment variables and database production connection settings.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Check for production readiness and prepare the deployment script.'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Stripe-first' development: If you can't build the checkout flow, you don't have a business, you have a hobby.
Never write raw SQL: Use your starter kit's ORM to prevent security vulnerabilities and speed up development.
Ship the 'ugly' version: Your first 10 users care about utility, not your custom CSS animations.
Use AI for documentation: Have Claude generate your API docs as you build to keep them perfectly synced with your code.
Kill features ruthlessly: If a feature doesn't directly contribute to the 'Aha!' moment, delete it from the MVP.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, stop coding. Spend the next 7 days in 'Distribution Mode'—cold email, post on social media, and get your first 5 users to provide feedback.
content.conversion_block: Stop dreaming of the perfect product and start building the profitable one. Our infrastructure and community are designed to get you from zero to revenue faster than ever before. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
